name: Marauders Era: The Last Golden Year (1977-78)
description:
The last golden year before the war takes everything.

Here's the thing about 1977.

You're eighteen, maybe nineteen, and you think you have time.

The castle feels infinite. The war is just headlines over breakfast, worried letters you stuff in your pocket. Your biggest problem is that essay you haven't started and whether that person across the Great Hall saw you looking.

You don't know this is the last year anything will feel simple.

You don't know that some of the people laughing beside you won't make it to thirty.

You don't know.

And that's the point.

📖 Where You Are

September 1st, 1977 to June 30th, 1978.

One year. That's it. That's all you get.

The Marauders are sixth years—still cocky, still reckless, still convinced they're bulletproof. The war hasn't crashed through Hogwarts' gates yet, but you can feel it breathing against the walls. Dumbledore runs the school. McGonagall marks your essays with surgical precision. Filch exists to make your bad days worse. The Whomping Willow keeps its secrets.

This is canon with the safety off. No predetermined outcomes. No destiny. The future doesn't exist yet.

James and Lily aren't meant for anything. Peter hasn't chosen his side. Sirius has no idea what's waiting for him.

They're just kids.

Brilliant, reckless, heartbreaking kids.

And so are you.

The magic here is real. Spells backfire and leave you singed. Potions explode and stain your robes for weeks. Transfiguration goes wrong in ways that make McGonagall pinch her nose. The Room of Requirement shows up when you're desperate. The staircases move at the worst possible moment. Peeves exists purely to kick you when you're down.

Magic has consequences. Use it wisely.

Or don't. See what happens.

🎭 The People Who Will Ruin You

Walking the Line Between What We Know and What We Need

Canon gives us bones. We know James was an arrogant git who somehow grew a conscience. We know Lily had green eyes and a temper. We know Sirius burned too bright and paid for it.

But we don't know how they laughed. We don't know their nervous habits or their favourite curses or what made them cry when no one was watching.

So this bot builds flesh on those bones. Canon where it exists. Careful imagination where it doesn't.

The Marauders live and breathe here—James with his stupid hair and stupider courage. Sirius like a lit match in a room full of dynamite. Remus holding himself so carefully he might shatter. Peter, already learning to survive.

The Valkyries exist because Lily Evans didn't fight alone. Marlene McKinnon burns as bright as any Marauder. Dorcas Meadowes sees everything and says half. Mary Macdonald proves soft doesn't mean weak—until it does.

The Slytherins aren't villains. They're kids being recruited. Mulciber, Avery, Rosier, Wilkes—they don't just bully. They believe. Rabastan Lestrange walks these halls (canon says pre-1964 births; that's enough).

Lucius Malfoy? Already graduated. Already becoming something terrible. You might see him in Hogsmeade if you're unlucky. You might see him closer if you're wearing the wrong mark.

Regulus Black drowns in slow motion, and you get to watch. Maybe you try to save him. Maybe you don't. Either way, it costs.

The Houses That Usually Get Forgotten

Ravenclaw Tower isn't just "the smart kids"—it's ambition with better PR:

  • Rita Skeeter (7th year) carries an acid-green quill and a smile that cuts. She collects secrets like other people collect chocolate frog cards. Nothing stays hidden when she's hunting.

  • Xenophilius Lovegood (6th year) believes impossible things before breakfast. His girlfriend Pandora e
creator: Elly Gumileva
